source_description: In an apartment living room, Monica approaches a baby carrier resting on a coffee table. Behind her, Joey, Chandler, and Ross eat pie in various parts of the room.
Monica bends toward the carrier and says, "Who? The Winnie, Minnie boys."
Monica lifts the baby from the carrier, holding him up. She smiles and says, "Oh, you the Winnie, winnie, minnie Winnie boy. Yes."
Monica lifts the baby from the carrier, holding him up. She smiles and says, "Oh, you the Winnie, winnie, minnie Winnie boy. Yes."
Monica lifts the baby from the carrier, holding him up. She smiles and says, "Oh, you the Winnie, winnie, minnie Winnie boy. Yes."
Monica sits the baby on her lap. The baby starts crying audibly.
Monica sits the baby on her lap. The baby starts crying audibly.
The baby continues crying.
Monica repeats, "Don't cry."
The baby cries. Monica turns her head toward the kitchen area.
The baby cries. Monica turns her head toward the kitchen area.
Monica asks, "Why is he still crying?"
Ross approaches from the kitchen area.
Ross dangles a set of keys near the baby's face, shaking them. The keys jingle. "Don't worry. Nothing that can't be solved by happy key," Ross says. The baby continues crying.
Ross dangles a set of keys near the baby's face, shaking them. The keys jingle. "Don't worry. Nothing that can't be solved by happy key," Ross says. The baby continues crying.
Joey holds a plate of pie and looks at Ross.
Joey says, "Yeah, I always feel much better when someone shakes jagged metal in my face."
Joey says, "Yeah, I always feel much better when someone shakes jagged metal in my face."
Joey says, "Yeah, I always feel much better when someone shakes jagged metal in my face."
Ross lifts the baby from Monica's lap.
Ross lifts the baby from Monica's lap.
Ross holds the baby against his chest. "Let me hold him for a sec. There we go," he says. The crying stops.
Ross holds the baby against his chest. "Let me hold him for a sec. There we go," he says. The crying stops.
Ross gently bounces the baby. Chandler walks over and stands beside Ross.
Ross says, "There we are."
Monica turns her gaze from Ross and Chandler to the empty space in front of her, then looks back.
Monica says, "Maybe it's me."
Ross says, "Don't be silly. Ben loves you. He's just being Mr. Cranky Pants."
Ross says, "Don't be silly. Ben loves you. He's just being Mr. Cranky Pants."
Ross says, "Don't be silly. Ben loves you. He's just being Mr. Cranky Pants."

Chandler looks at Ross. Ross looks at Chandler.
Chandler continues, "Lovely girl. Kind of moody."
Chandler continues, "Lovely girl. Kind of moody."

Ross lowers the baby toward Monica and says, "There we go. All better. There's my little boy."
Ross lowers the baby toward Monica and says, "There we go. All better. There's my little boy."
Monica takes the baby into her arms. The baby immediately resumes crying loudly.
Monica holds the baby with an open-mouthed expression as the baby cries. Chandler and Ross watch.
Monica holds the baby with an open-mouthed expression as the baby cries. Chandler and Ross watch.
Chandler raises his finger and steps forward.
Chandler raises his finger and steps forward.

Chandler hands the baby back to Monica. The instant Monica holds the baby, the loud crying resumes.

Monica raises her hands and lets them fall. Chandler turns and walks back toward the kitchen area.

Monica stands up, carrying the crying baby to the carrier on the table. She says, "He hates me. My nephew hates me. I'm unemployed and my nephew hates me."
Monica stands up, carrying the crying baby to the carrier on the table. She says, "He hates me. My nephew hates me. I'm unemployed and my nephew hates me."
